commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7aa72463a0f4844b84dd93417c3ef562f12c2392)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a separate `ReplyOperation.explicitAbortSignal` and changes required preflight compaction to use that signal instead of the raw reply operation abort signal.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +87. Total +104 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes `replyOperation.abortSignal` into required budget/preflight compaction and the safety wrapper races that abort before the configured timeout; I did not live-run the reporter's slow vLLM/Qwen setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review and contributor revision are needed because the patch must preserve user/RPC/restart aborts and provide real slow-preflight proof before landing.

target.title: [codex] fix(reply): let preflight compaction use compaction timeout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95553. Required preflight compaction could inherit the outer reply-operation abort signal, so a short reply lifecycle abort window could cancel compaction before the configured compaction timeout had a chance to govern the required preflight work.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps required preflight compaction bounded by the compaction layer while still preserving explicit cancellation. `ReplyOperation` now exposes `explicitAbortSignal`, which aborts for user stop and restart cancellation but does not abort for upstream reply lifecycle cancellation. Required preflight compaction forwards that explicit signal instead of the raw `replyOperation.abortSignal`.

The patch is intentionally narrow: normal reply runs still use the existing operation abort signal, while only required preflight compaction avoids the lifecycle timeout signal that caused #95553.

## User Impact

When a reply needs preflight compaction, users should see compaction complete or fail according to `compaction.timeoutSeconds`, instead of being prematurely canceled by the reply lifecycle abort path. If the user explicitly stops the run or the run is restarted, the preflight compaction still fails closed through the explicit cancellation signal.

This PR is split out from #95563 to keep the P1 behavior fix reviewable on its own.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts` passed: 2 auto-reply files / 66 tests and 1 agents file / 22 tests.
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
target.author: yu-xin-c

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
